title: dalbergia brachystachya
intro: Dalbergia brachystachya, also known as East Indian rosewood, is a species of flowering plant in the legume family, Fabaceae. Traditionally, the plant's bark and roots have been used in Ayurvedic medicine, exhibiting medicinal properties such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Dalbergia brachystachya include its potential to aid in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions, as well as its use as an antiseptic. Herbal preparations of Dalbergia brachystachya can be found in various forms, including powders, decoctions, and infusions, which are often used to make medicines and tonics.
